description: Source character is parent of target character
notes: Use with reverse relationship child_of where useful

description: Source character is child of target character
notes: Useful where the child is the main focus

description: Characters are siblings
Characters are married or treated as spouses

description: Characters oppose each other
notes: Can include war rivalry or personal hostility

description: Source character kills target character
notes: Use only where the tradition is clear enough

description: Source character abducts target character
Used where the tradition varies between abduction and elopement

description: Source character helps target character
notes: Can include divine support or practical help

description: Source character hosts target character
Source character detains target character
notes: Used for Calypso and similar stories

description: Source character detains or transforms others
Source character enters a realm ruled by target character
Source character rides or uses target creature
Source character creates structure for target character
Target character emerges from source character or event
Characters are linked but relationship is not simple
Source character advises target character
Source character warns or reveals truth to target character

description: Used where family ownership or control is awkward to classify
